datasync.task.taskreportconfig.CfnAbout:
Specifies how you want to configure a task report, which provides detailed information about for your AWS DataSync transfer.

For more information, see [Task reports](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/datasync/latest/userguide/creating-task-reports.html).

datasync.task.taskreportconfig.Destinati.Description: Specifies the Amazon S3 bucket where DataSync uploads your task report. For more information, see [Task reports](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/datasync/latest/userguide/creating-task-reports.html#task-report-access).

datasync.task.taskreportconfig.ObjectVer.Description: Specifies whether your task report includes the new version of each object transferred into an S3 bucket. This only applies if you [enable versioning on your bucket](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/manage-versioning-examples.html). Keep in mind that setting this to `INCLUDE` can increase the duration of your task execution.

datasync.task.taskreportconfig.OutputTyp.Description: Specifies the type of task report that you want:  +  `SUMMARY_ONLY`: Provides necessary details about your task, including the number of files, objects, and directories transferred and transfer duration.+  `STANDARD`: Provides complete details about your task, including a full list of files, objects, and directories that were transferred, skipped, verified, and more.

datasync.task.taskreportconfig.Overrides.Description: Customizes the reporting level for aspects of your task report. For example, your report might generally only include errors, but you could specify that you want a list of successes and errors just for the files that DataSync attempted to delete in your destination location.

datasync.task.taskreportconfig.ReportLev.Description: Specifies whether you want your task report to include only what went wrong with your transfer or a list of what succeeded and didn't.  +  `ERRORS_ONLY`: A report shows what DataSync was unable to transfer, skip, verify, and delete.+  `SUCCESSES_AND_ERRORS`: A report shows what DataSync was able and unable to transfer, skip, verify, and delete.
